back_translation: There was a man named Lazarus who was sick. He lived in Bethany village, the village of Mary and Martha her older sister.
This Mary is the one who later anointed the body of the Lord Jesus with perfume, and wiped His feet with her hair. Her younger brother was Lazarus, who was sick.
The two sisters therefore sent someone to tell Him, “My Lord, the one You love is sick.”
When Jesus heard this, He said, “This sickness will not end in death, but is for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may receive glory through this sickness.”
Jesus loved Martha, her younger sister, and Lazarus.
When He heard that Lazarus was sick, He stayed there longer for two more days.
After that, He said to His disciples, “Let us return to the region of Judea.”
The disciples said to Him, “Rabbi, the Jewish leaders were just now seeking to stone You. Will You go back there again?”
Jesus answered, “Are there not twelve hours in a day? If anyone walks during the daytime, he will not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
But if anyone walks at night, he will stumble, because there is no light in him.”
When He had said that, He said to them, “Lazarus our friend has fallen asleep, but I am going to wake him up.”
The disciples therefore said to Him, “My Lord, if he is asleep, he will recover.”
Jesus said this referring to Lazarus's death, but the disciples thought He meant ordinary sleep.
Jesus then told them clearly, “Lazarus has died,
and for your benefit I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. Let us go to him.”
Thomas, who is called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, “Let us go together, so that we may die along with Him.”
When Jesus arrived, He found that Lazarus had been buried in the tomb already four days.
Bethany was near Jerusalem, about three kilometers.
Many of the Jewish people had come to visit Martha and Mary to console them about the death of their younger brother.
When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went out to meet Him, but Mary still sat in the house.
Martha said to Jesus, “My Lord, if You had been here, my younger brother would not have died.
But even now, I know that whatever You ask of God, God will give to You.”
Jesus said to her, “Your younger brother will rise again.”
Martha said to Him, “I know that he will rise again at the resurrection on the last day.”
Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in Me, even if he dies, he will still be alive,
and everyone who is alive and believes in Me will not die forever. Do you believe this?”
She said to Him, “I believe, my Lord. I have come to believe that You are the Christ, the Son of God, the One who has come into the world.”
When Martha had said that, she went back and called Mary her younger sister privately, saying, “The Teacher has arrived, and is calling for you.”
When Mary heard this, she got up immediately and hurried to Him.
Jesus had not yet entered the village, but was still at the place where Martha had met Him.
The Jewish people who were with her in the house consoling her, when they saw Mary get up and go out at once, followed her, supposing she was going to the tomb to weep there.
When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw Him, she prostrated herself at His feet, saying to Him, “My Lord, if You had been here, my younger brother would not have died.”
When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jewish people who came with her also weeping, He was deeply moved within Himself and was troubled.
And He said, “Where have you laid him?” They said to Him, “My Lord, please come and see.”
Jesus shed tears.
The Jewish people therefore said, “See how greatly He loved him!”
But some of them said, “The one who opened the eyes of the blind man — could He not have kept this man from dying?”
Jesus, deeply moved within Himself again, came to the tomb. It was a cave with a stone laid across the cave-mouth.
Jesus said, “Take away the stone.” Martha, the older sister of the deceased, said to Him, “My Lord, by now the body must stink, for he has been dead four days.”
Jesus said to her, “Did I not tell you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?”
So they took the stone away. Jesus lifted up His face and said, “My Father, I thank You that You have heard Me.
I knew that You always hear Me, but I say this because of the people standing around, so that they may believe that You sent Me.”
When He had said that, He cried out in a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”
The one who had died came out, his hands and feet bound with linen cloth, and his face wrapped with a face-covering cloth. Jesus said to them, “Unwrap the cloth and let him go.”
Therefore many of the Jewish people who came to visit Mary and saw what He did, believed in Him.
But some of them went to the Pharisees and reported to them what Jesus had done.
The chief priests and the Pharisees therefore convened the Sanhedrin and said, “What shall we do, for this man is performing many signs?
If we let him alone like this, all will believe in him, and the Romans will come and destroy both our place and our nation.”
But one of them, named Caiaphas, who was high priest in that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all.
Do you not realize that it is good for you that one man die for the people, rather than that the whole nation perish?”
Caiaphas did not say this on his own, but because he was high priest in that year, he prophesied that Jesus would lay down His life for that nation,
and not only for that nation, but also to gather the children of God who were scattered, to make them one.
From that day on, they plotted to kill Him.
Therefore Jesus no longer went about openly among the Jewish leaders, but went away from there to a region near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, and stayed there with His disciples.
The Jewish Passover festival was approaching, and many people from the countryside went up to Jerusalem before the Passover, to purify themselves.
They were therefore looking for Jesus, and said among themselves while standing in the temple courts, “What do you think? Will He come to this festival?”
The chief priests and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where He was, they should report so that they could arrest Him.
